mission.title: Install and Configure MongoDB Community Operator
mission.description: The MongoDB Community Operator allows you to deploy and manage MongoDB Community instances on Kubernetes. It simplifies the deployment process and provides lifecycle management for MongoDB clusters. However, note that this operator is deprecated, and users are encouraged to migrate to the new MongoDB Kubernetes Operator.

steps.title: Add the MongoDB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the MongoDB Community Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add mongodb https://mongodb.github.io/mongodb-kubernetes-operator

helm repo update

```

> This adds the correct repository for the MongoDB Community Operator.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the MongoDB Community Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the MongoDB Community Operator in the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace mongodb

helm install mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb --version 0.13.0

```

> Creates a new namespace for MongoDB resources.
> Installs the MongoDB Community Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a MongoDB resource
steps.description:
This step creates a MongoDB cluster resource using a YAML configuration file.

```bash

cat <<EOF > mongodb-cluster.yaml
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.0
  persistent: true
EOF

kubectl apply -f mongodb-cluster.yaml

```

> Creates a YAML file for a MongoDB cluster with 3 members.
> Applies the configuration to create the MongoDB cluster.
commands:
cat <<EOF > mongodb-cluster.yaml
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.0
  persistent: true
EOF

steps.title: Verify the MongoDB cluster
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the MongoDB cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb

kubectl get pods -n mongodb

```

> Lists the MongoDB resources in the specified namespace.
> Verifies that all MongoDB pods are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the MongoDB Community Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the MongoDB Community Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all MongoDB data and resources in the namespace.

```bash

helm uninstall mongodb-community-operator --namespace mongodb

kubectl delete namespace mongodb

```

> Uninstalls the MongoDB Community Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the MongoDB Community Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your MongoDB data. This step will upgrade the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb -o yaml > mongodb-backup.yaml

helm upgrade mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb --version 0.13.0

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb

```

> Backs up the current MongoDB configuration.
> Upgrades the MongoDB Community Operator.
> Verifies the MongoDB resource status after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb -o yaml > mongodb-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create MongoDB resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the YAML configuration is incorrect or missing required fields.

**Fix:**
Check the YAML file for syntax errors and ensure all required fields are present.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the MongoDB pods are failing to start due to configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n mongodb' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: MongoDB cluster is not initializing
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the persistent volume claims are not bound correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the status of PVCs with 'kubectl get pvc -n mongodb' and ensure they are bound.

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'release already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that a release with the same name already exists in the namespace.

**Fix:**
Use 'helm uninstall mongodb-community-operator --namespace mongodb' to remove the existing release before reinstalling.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved logging capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: No new deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.12.0, ensure to review the new logging configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the MongoDB cluster listed with 'kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb' and all pods in 'Running' state.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF > mongodb-cluster.yaml
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.0
  persistent: true
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
